title: Boundary Data
text: Use jpmap_data_dir() find default local data directory.

title: Boundary Data
text: Boundary files can built Japan’s MLIT National Land Numerical Information N03 administrative area data. 2024 source archive 583 MB, command intentionally user-run step.

title: Boundary Data
text: building placing GeoPackage data directory, inspect available years : expected file name pattern jpmap_boundaries_YYYY.gpkg, layers named prefectures municipalities.

title: FAQ
text: Japan spans long north-south east-west range. geographically literal map can make smaller islands difficult see. jpmap follows usmap idea using insets important regions remain visible ordinary plots. Use inset = FALSE, inset = "okinawa", inset = "ogasawara" want exclude one transported island groups.

title: FAQ
text: package includes code building reading boundary data, large boundary files kept outside package. Use jpmap_build_data() build local GeoPackage MLIT N03 administrative area data.

title: FAQ
text: API supports regions = "prefectures" regions = "municipalities".

what: Can I add my own points or lines?
title: FAQ
text: Yes. Use jpmap_transform() data frame, sf object, sfc geometry vector, add plot_jpmap() map ggplot2 layers.

title: Plot Maps
text: Join data common administrative code name column set values column mapped fill color.
code: population <- data.frame(   pref_code = c("13", "27", "47"),   population = c(14000000, 8800000, 1460000) )  plot_jpmap(   "prefectures",   data = population,   values = "population" ) +   scale_fill_continuous(low = "#F7F1E4", high = "#782F40")

title: Transform Data
code: library(jpmap)  places <- data.frame(   place = c("Tokyo", "Naha", "Ogasawara"),   lon = c(139.767, 127.681, 142.191),   lat = c(35.681, 26.212, 27.094) )  jpmap_transform(places, output_names = c("x", "y")) #>       place     lon    lat         x         y #> 1     Tokyo 139.767 35.681  205212.3 -199413.7 #> 2      Naha 127.681 26.212 -669566.3 -709667.3 #> 3 Ogasawara 142.191 27.094 -301154.6 -827881.3

title: Transform Data
text: data sf object, jpmap_transform() returns sf object. Use inset = FALSE want projected Japan CRS Okinawa/Ogasawara inset movement. can also move one island group.
code: pts <- sf::st_as_sf(   places,   coords = c("lon", "lat"),   crs = 4326,   remove = FALSE )  jpmap_transform(pts) #> Simple feature collection with 3 features and 3 fields #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -669566.3 ymin: -827881.3 xmax: 205212.3 ymax: -199413.7 #> Projected CRS: +proj=laea +lat_0=37.5 +lon_0=137.5 +x_0=0 +y_0=0 +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs #>       place     lon    lat                    geometry #> 1     Tokyo 139.767 35.681  POINT (205212.3 -199413.7) #> 2      Naha 127.681 26.212 POINT (-669566.3 -709667.3) #> 3 Ogasawara 142.191 27.094 POINT (-301154.6 -827881.3) jpmap_transform(places, output_names = c("x", "y"), inset = FALSE) #>       place     lon    lat         x          y #> 1     Tokyo 139.767 35.681  205212.3  -199413.7 #> 2      Naha 127.681 26.212 -983633.2 -1201884.3 #> 3 Ogasawara 142.191 27.094  466806.8 -1141584.2 jpmap_transform(places, output_names = c("x", "y"), inset = "okinawa") #>       place     lon    lat         x          y #> 1     Tokyo 139.767 35.681  205212.3  -199413.7 #> 2      Naha 127.681 26.212 -669566.3  -709667.3 #> 3 Ogasawara 142.191 27.094  466806.8 -1141584.2 jpmap_transform(places, output_names = c("x", "y"), inset = "ogasawara") #>       place     lon    lat         x          y #> 1     Tokyo 139.767 35.681  205212.3  -199413.7 #> 2      Naha 127.681 26.212 -983633.2 -1201884.3 #> 3 Ogasawara 142.191 27.094 -301154.6  -827881.3

title: Authors and Citation

title: jpmap
text: Use jpmap_transform() put user-supplied longitude latitude data projected coordinate system used plot_jpmap().
code: library(jpmap)  points <- data.frame(   place = c("Tokyo", "Naha", "Ogasawara"),   lon = c(139.767, 127.681, 142.191),   lat = c(35.681, 26.212, 27.094) )  jpmap_transform(points)

title: jpmap
text: package expects administrative boundary data GeoPackage files named jpmap_boundaries_YYYY.gpkg. can build one Japan’s MLIT National Land Numerical Information N03 administrative area data: 2024 N03 source archive large, 583 MB, explicit user-run step rather something package installation. generated file written jpmap_data_dir() default contains two layers: prefectures municipalities data available, jp_map() returns sf objects plot_jpmap() returns ordinary ggplot2 maps. package also includes small Natural Earth prefecture layer examples website figures. Detailed municipal boundaries still require jpmap_build_data().

title: jpmap
text: Two public-source sample datasets included: jp_prefecture_gdp: 2021 prefecture GDP per capita values. jp_us_military_bases: selected U.S. military installations Japan coordinates public approximate personnel figures available. See “U.S. Military Bases Prefecture GDP” article example keeps installation locations separate regional command-level personnel figures.

title: jpmap
text: Build pkgdown site locally :

title: Retrieve Japan Map Data — jp_map
text: Reads Japan administrative boundary data returns sf object.

title: Retrieve Japan Map Data — jp_map
code: jp_map(   regions = c("prefectures", "prefecture", "municipalities", "municipality"),   include = c(),   exclude = c(),   data_year = NULL,   inset = TRUE,   data_dir = NULL )

title: Retrieve Japan Map Data — jp_map
text: regions Boundary level: prefectures municipalities. include Regions include code, English name, Japanese name. exclude Regions exclude code, English name, Japanese name. data_year Boundary data year. newest available year used default. inset Inset behavior. Use TRUE move Okinawa Ogasawara, FALSE movement, character vector containing "okinawa" /"ogasawara" move selected island groups. data_dir Optional directory containing jpmap_boundaries_YYYY.gpkg.

title: Retrieve Japan Map Data — jp_map
text: sf data frame.

what: Join Data to a jpmap Map — jp_map_with_data

text: Joins user data Japan map object.

title: Join Data to a jpmap Map — jp_map_with_data
code: jp_map_with_data(map, data, values = NULL, by = NULL)

title: Join Data to a jpmap Map — jp_map_with_data
text: map sf object returned jp_map(). data data frame containing matching administrative code name column. values Optional value column check joining. Optional join column. omitted, jpmap guesses common columns.

title: Join Data to a jpmap Map — jp_map_with_data
text: sf object.

what: GDP per capita by Japanese prefecture — jp_prefecture_gdp
title: GDP per capita by Japanese prefecture — jp_prefecture_gdp
text: sample data frame containing 2021 prefecture GDP per capita values.

title: GDP per capita by Japanese prefecture — jp_prefecture_gdp
text: data frame 47 rows 6 variables: pref_code Two-digit Japanese prefecture code. prefecture Prefecture name English. year Reference year. gdp_per_capita_jpy GDP per capita Japanese yen. gdp_per_capita_usd_ppp GDP per capita U.S. dollars PPP. source Source note.

title: GDP per capita by Japanese prefecture — jp_prefecture_gdp
text: OECD 2021 regional GDP per-capita values tabulated https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Japanese_prefectures_by_GDP_per_capita.

what: Selected U.S. military installations in Japan — jp_us_military_bases
title: Selected U.S. military installations in Japan — jp_us_military_bases
text: sample data frame containing selected U.S. military installations Japan longitude/latitude coordinates public approximate personnel figures figures clear source.

title: Selected U.S. military installations in Japan — jp_us_military_bases
text: data frame 17 rows 12 variables: base Installation command name. branch Primary U.S. military branch. prefecture Japanese prefecture. municipality Municipality municipalities. lon Longitude WGS84. lat Latitude WGS84. personnel Approximate public personnel figure, available. personnel_scope public figure counts. personnel_geography Whether figure describes installation, regional total, command, broader installation community. personnel_is_base_specific Whether personnel interpreted base-specific figure. source_url Source URL. note Caveat use examples.

title: Selected U.S. military installations in Japan — jp_us_military_bases
text: values intended examples official personnel accounting. rows describe broader installation, regional, command, community populations; see personnel_scope personnel_geography.

what: jpmap: Japan Maps with Insets for Okinawa and Ogasawara — jpmap-package
title: jpmap: Japan Maps with Insets for Okinawa and Ogasawara — jpmap-package
text: Tools drawing maps Japan prefecture municipal boundaries. API mirrors main workflow usmap package.

what: Coordinate Reference System for jpmap — jpmap_crs
title: Coordinate Reference System for jpmap — jpmap_crs
text: Returns projected coordinate reference system used jpmap.

title: Coordinate Reference System for jpmap — jpmap_crs
Coordinate Reference System for jpmap — jpmap_crs
text: sf::st_crs() object.

title: Manage jpmap Boundary Data — jpmap_data
text: Helpers locating building GeoPackage boundary data used jpmap.

title: Manage jpmap Boundary Data — jpmap_data
code: jpmap_data_dir(create = TRUE)  available_jpmap_data(data_dir = NULL)  jpmap_build_data(   year = 2024,   destdir = jpmap_data_dir(),   url = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE,   simplify_tolerance = NULL )

title: Manage jpmap Boundary Data — jpmap_data
text: create Whether create default data directory. data_dir Optional directory scan boundary data. year Boundary data year. destdir Directory generated GeoPackage written. url Optional source URL. default, MLIT N03 URL constructed. overwrite Whether overwrite existing GeoPackage. quiet Whether suppress messages download spatial reads/writes. simplify_tolerance Optional tolerance passed sf::st_simplify().

title: Manage jpmap Boundary Data — jpmap_data
text: jpmap_data_dir() returns path, available_jpmap_data() returns data frame, jpmap_build_data() invisibly returns generated file.

what: Transform Data to the jpmap Projection — jpmap_transform
title: Transform Data to the jpmap Projection — jpmap_transform
text: Transforms geographic data jpmap projected coordinate system. inset = TRUE, Okinawa Ogasawara moved visible inset locations. Use inset = "okinawa" inset = "ogasawara" transport one island group.

title: Transform Data to the jpmap Projection — jpmap_transform
code: jpmap_transform(data, input_names = c("lon", "lat"), output_names = input_names, inset = TRUE)

title: Transform Data to the jpmap Projection — jpmap_transform
text: data sf object, sfc geometry vector, data frame. input_names Longitude latitude column names data frames. output_names Output coordinate column names data frames. inset Inset behavior. Use TRUE move Okinawa Ogasawara, FALSE movement, character vector containing "okinawa" /"ogasawara" move selected island groups.

title: Transform Data to the jpmap Projection — jpmap_transform
text: object general type data.

title: Transform Data to the jpmap Projection — jpmap_transform
Plot a Japan Map — plot_jpmap
text: Plots Japan prefecture municipal boundaries using ggplot2.

title: Plot a Japan Map — plot_jpmap
code: plot_jpmap(   regions = c("prefectures", "prefecture", "municipalities", "municipality"),   include = c(),   exclude = c(),   data = data.frame(),   values = NULL,   labels = FALSE,   label_color = "black",   data_year = NULL,   inset = TRUE,   data_dir = NULL,   color = "white",   linewidth = 0.2,   ... )

title: Plot a Japan Map — plot_jpmap
text: regions Boundary level: prefectures municipalities. include Regions include code, English name, Japanese name. exclude Regions exclude code, English name, Japanese name. data Optional data frame join map. values Column name data use fill variable. labels Whether draw region labels. label_color Label text color. data_year Boundary data year. inset Inset behavior. Use TRUE move Okinawa Ogasawara, FALSE movement, character vector containing "okinawa" /"ogasawara" move selected island groups. data_dir Optional directory containing jpmap_boundaries_YYYY.gpkg. color Boundary line color. linewidth Boundary line width. ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::geom_sf().

title: Plot a Japan Map — plot_jpmap
text: ggplot2 plot.

title: jpmap 0.0.0.9000
text: Initial development version. Added usmap-style API Japan maps: jp_map(), plot_jpmap(), jpmap_transform(), jpmap_crs(). Added support prefecture municipal boundary layers. Added inset transformations Okinawa Ogasawara, support selecting island groups transport via inset. Added pkgdown website configuration using Florida State University garnet gold colors. Added sample GDP-per-capita U.S. military base datasets plus website vignette showing Japan map. military-base example now separates installation locations regional, command-level, base-specific public personnel figures.
